features.evidence: The 16" OLED display is the star, exceptionally vivid and noticeably brighter than other laptops I’ve used, which makes both creative work and games pop. ConclusionOverall, this is a balanced, premium-feeling laptop with a phenomenal OLED screen, straightforward setup, and solid performance for creators and casual gaming.

ProsStunning, very bright 16" OLED, fantastic for games and creative workQuick, easy setup; Windows Hello face unlock works smoothlyResponsive performance; plays Helldivers at low–medium settings without issuesHandles 3D scanning/processing well for project workPremium build and feelConsKeyboard feels slightly off-center (minor adjustment period)Would benefit from more RAM for the priceFans can ramp under load (expected with gaming/creator tasks)Potential drawbacks/concernsIf you plan to run heavy creative suites plus many browser tabs, consider a higher-RAM configuration or be mindful of memory use. My main wish is for more onboard memory at this price,16GB works,

Setup was quick (about 30 minutes), Windows Hello face sign-in worked right away, and overall responsiveness has been snappy.

For gaming, I saw smooth performance in Helldivers on low–medium settings. ConclusionOverall, this is a balanced, premium-feeling laptop with a phenomenal OLED screen, straightforward setup, and solid performance for creators and casual gaming.

I cannot restart unless I hold the power button down for a few minutes to discharge the battery then recharge again. you may want to set up battery and display profiles to balance battery life vs. visual punch.

If you can live with the slightly off-center keyboard and wish for a bit more RAM, it’s a compelling pick for a bright, color-rich portable that pulls double duty for work and play. The keyboard layout feels slightly off-center; it threw me for a bit at first
All the other basic tasks work as expected - Meet/Teams/Zoom work - the camera resolution is fine and no one has complained about the audio yet.
